tweets.text:
Quasar Markets | Fed Signal Breakdown
The Federal Reserve just delivered a subtle but important shift in tone.

Several FOMC members would have supported “two-sided” language on the rate path. Translation: policy is no longer framed as a one-directional easing trajectory. The Committee wants optionality.

At the same time, several officials indicated that additional cuts remain possible, if inflation declines as expected. That conditional clause matters. The easing bias is data-contingent, not forecast-driven.

However, most participants cautioned that disinflation could prove slower than anticipated. This reinforces the Fed’s core risk: services inflation, wage stickiness, and potential supply shocks could delay the glide path back to target.

Market Implications

• Front-end yields remain highly CPI/PCE sensitive
• Equity multiples stay tethered to real rate volatility
• USD supported if U.S. inflation remains firmer than peers
• Gold benefits from rate-cut optionality, but timing is uncertain Quasar Markets | Fed Signal Breakdown

However, most participants cautioned that disinflation could prove slower than anticipated. This reinforces the Fed’s core risk: services inflation, wage stickiness, and potential supply shocks could delay the glide path back to target.

tweets.text:
Quasar Markets Newsletter TL;DR

Japan's prime minister reelection and HSBC's upgrade of L’Oréal highlight global market shifts, while JPMorgan favors international stocks over American ones.

Market Movers:
NVIDIA ($NVDA), Ondas Holdings ($ONDS), Tesla ($TSLA) show varied activity; IBRX gains on Saudi approval, ONDS rebounds with German police deal.

Corporate Actions:
Peter Thiel's Founders Fund exits ETHZilla; MoonLake stock surges on takeover speculation.

Economic Indicators:
No significant updates.

Market Sentiment:
European stocks poised for gains; JPMorgan sees potential in international equities, contrasting with American market outlook. Quasar Markets Newsletter TL;DR

tweets.text:
Most leadership doesn’t happen at the top.

It happens in the middle.

The only truly autonomous operator in a business is the founder.
Everyone else is leading through influence, alignment, and constraint.

I’ve come to believe becoming a strong CEO requires first learning how to lead without ultimate authority.

Middle management isn’t a stepping stone.
It’s a proving ground.

That realization has shaped more of my thinking than most frameworks ever did. Most leadership doesn’t happen at the top.

I’ve come to believe becoming a strong CEO requires first learning how to lead without ultimate authority.

Middle management isn’t a stepping stone.
It’s a proving ground.

That realization has shaped more of my thinking than most frameworks ever did.

tweets.text:
📊 When markets get volatile, pricing inefficiencies don’t last long, and understanding the mechanics behind options pricing can reveal opportunities a lot of traders miss.

Put/Call Parity isn’t just theory…it’s the framework that keeps the options market logically aligned when fear, greed, and volatility collide.

On today’s MarketMinds, Guest Host Mike Shorr is continuing our deep dive into Put/Call Parity, and how it applies in real-world trading conditions.

Here’s what’s on tap:
-A practical refresher on the Put/Call Parity relationship
-What parity can reveal about synthetic positions and hidden exposures
-How traders can spot potential mispricings or arbitrage-style setups

Mike goes LIVE @ 1 PM ET. Watch for FREE on our YouTube channel: https://www.youtube.com/live/wVWdaGgb8ZQ?si=Y2evtMPELh7AQ5pB 📊 When markets get volatile, pricing inefficiencies don’t last long, and understanding the mechanics behind options pricing can reveal opportunities a lot of traders miss.

tweets.text:
Numbers from our first 30 days of AI outreach:

📊 47% email open rate (industry avg: 21%)
📊 17% reply rate (industry avg: 3%)
📊 5% closing rate from email alone

The secret was not better subject lines.

It was sending fewer emails to the right people at the right time.

100 targeted emails > 10,000 spray and pray.

Quality of targeting is the only metric that matters in outbound. 🎯
